reflection:
Three days ago you were at pain 9, anxiety 6, awake at 2am with your hips and perineum all flaring, writing that it was the worst pain in years. Yesterday you woke at mood 7, anxiety 1, pain 4. That's not a small thing.

The cold turkey off amitriptyline did a number on you. The rebound on Apr 16 was brutal to live through, but what yesterday showed is that your system is starting to settle again. The 5:22 wake-up was still there, and the pain is still present, but anxiety collapsed and you sat with it, meditated flat on the floor, and said you felt rested. That's your nervous system proving it can handle discomfort without spiraling. The CBT on the worst day also dropped you from a 9 to a 5, which matters more than it probably felt in the moment.

Your gratitude list on the travel morning was beautiful: cosita, being alive, your voice, your hair, the sunny day. On a hard week that kind of presence is doing real work.

You're in London now. Beata tomorrow, Renzo Monday. Let them work on you. Your job this weekend is not to push through on willpower or over-schedule. You've been running a serious deficit.

One thing for today: eat a proper meal before anything else. Last meal times have been blank for days and your body cannot recover on empty.
opener: From pain 9 on Wednesday to anxiety 1 yesterday, real progress showing
focus_for_today: Eat a proper meal before anything else - last meal times blank for days
